data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T194134-d54f4722/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T194134-d54f4722/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T194134-d54f4722/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T194134-d54f4722/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T194134-d54f4722/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-c54b817c">
    <title>agent: clear default profile in pre-dispatch lint and post-attempt triage hooks</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`ddx work` without flags produces `lint hook: dispatch-failure: ResolveRoute: no viable routing candidate for pins min_power=7 max_power=8: N candidates rejected` because the pre-dispatch lint hook inherits `rcfg.Profile()="default"`, and fizeau's "default" profile pins `min_power=7,max_power=8`. Operators whose providers fall outside that band — local providers (power<=6) or frontier cloud providers via openrouter (power=9) — see every candidate rejected and the agent exits in <1s with no_evidence_produced. The post-attempt triage hook has the same latent bug. Pre-claim intake already sidesteps this with `ClearProfile: true`. Repro: in this repo with no flags, `ddx work --once` will emit the dispatch-failure event on the next claimed bead — see `.ddx/executions/20260508T173611-c6069449/manifest.json` events for a captured instance.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/routing_profile.go:11` normalizes empty profile to `"default"`.
- `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:186-189` flows `rcfg.Profile()` into `ServiceExecuteRequest.Profile` when no `ProfileOverride` is set and `ClearProfile` is false.
- `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132` constructs the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime` without `ClearProfile` or `ProfileOverride`, so the lint hook's request carries `Profile="default"`.
- `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118` has the same omission.
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:96-106` demonstrates the fix shape: `ClearProfile: true`.
- Downstream: fizeau's `internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml` pins the `default` profile to `min_power: 7, max_power: 8`; `service_routing.go` `routePowerPolicyForRequest` overlays that on every request whose `Profile` field is set.

PROPOSED FIX
- In `NewPreDispatchLintHook` (`cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:128-132`), set `ClearProfile: true` on the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime`. Lint is a mechanical scoring task; no profile constraint is needed.
- In `NewPostAttemptTriageHook` (`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:114-118`), set `ClearProfile: true` for the same reason.
- Do not change `executeOnService`, `NormalizeRoutingProfile`, or any caller that legitimately uses Profile="default" with explicit `--harness/--model` overrides (those bypass the profile bound via fizeau's `routePowerBoundsForRequest`).
- Do not touch `NewPreClaimIntakeHook`; it already does the right thing.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change `NormalizeRoutingProfile` or the meaning of "default" — execute-bead and `ddx run` callers still rely on it as the default request shape and supply their own `--harness`/`--model`/`--profile` to bypass the bound.
- Do not modify fizeau's catalog.
- Do not add a new global setting; the fix is local to the two hook constructors.

PARENT + DEPS
No parent. No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `NewPreDispatchLintHook` (cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go) constructs the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime` with `ClearProfile: true` so `executeOnService` does not flow `rcfg.Profile()` into `ServiceExecuteRequest.Profile`.
2. `NewPostAttemptTriageHook` (cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go) constructs the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime` with `ClearProfile: true` for the same reason.
3. New test `TestPreDispatchLintHook_ClearsProfileSoDefaultPowerBoundsDoNotApply` in `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook_test.go` builds the hook with a `ResolvedConfig` whose `Profile()=="default"` and a service stub that captures `ServiceExecuteRequest`, then asserts the dispatched request's `Profile` field is empty.
4. New test `TestPostAttemptTriageHook_ClearsProfileSoDefaultPowerBoundsDoNotApply` in `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook_test.go` does the same for the triage hook.
5. Existing `TestLintHook_*` tests in `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook_test.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_routing_preflight_test.go` still pass.
6. Existing `TestTriageHook_*` tests in `cli/internal/agent/triage_hook_test.go` still pass.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestPreDispatchLintHook_ClearsProfile|TestPostAttemptTriageHook_ClearsProfile|TestLintHook|TestTriageHook" -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/...` green.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:routing, kind:bug, reliability, operator-ux, spec:FEAT-010</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="81a63c0f490396dd7ea11a3fe1925c176cbd1e76" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T194134-d54f4722"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All AC items verified:

1. `lint_hook.go` now has `ClearProfile: true` on the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime`
2. `triage_hook.go` now has `ClearProfile: true` on the dispatched `AgentRunRuntime`
3. `TestPreDispatchLintHook_ClearsProfileSoDefaultPowerBoundsDoNotApply` passes — uses a service stub with `Profile()=="default"`, asserts `svc.lastReq.Profile == ""`
4. `TestPostAttemptTriageHook_ClearsProfileSoDefaultPowerBoundsDoNotApply` passes — same pattern for triage hook
5-8. All existing `TestLintHook_*` and `TestTriageHook_*` tests pass
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes (no violations)
